scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from manual agricultural labor is a direct physical and economic relief.
scores.generality_reasoning: Labor status is a broad category defining daily life and social class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: As a class definition for Guardians, the exemption is structural and lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Guardianship is an occupational class, often semi-hereditary but requiring training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle refers to contemporary practices in Sparta and current theory.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a factual measure 'even now' attempted by Spartans.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Guardians are defined by protection/governance, not strictly by 'not farming'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Inheritance of estates involves tangible land and wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property inheritance is a foundational societal axis for economic survival.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inherited estates provide lifelong wealth and legal standing.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being an 'heir' is defined by birth/lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Uses the phrase 'for now' to describe current societal reality.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the current division of estates as an observed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: An heir, by definition, is someone with the right to inherit property.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to bear arms involves physical possession of weapons and military status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bearing arms is a major category linked to political power and defense.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Typically held through adult years of citizen service.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the constitution of existing or proposed contemporary republics.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of how a specific 'republic' type is constituted.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently mean one is armed (e.g., naval groups).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for high office (Ephorate) provides direct political power/authority.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically concerns one high office, but recurring annually.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility lasts as long as one is part of the 'common people'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'common people' (demos) is a structural birth-based class.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the actual functioning of the Spartan constitution.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a known fact of the Spartan system.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Membership in the common people does not automatically entail office eligibility.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participation in common meals provides daily physical subsistence/food.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring access to a specific social and dietary institution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong participation for adult male citizens in Sparta.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the existing Spartan syssitia.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a factual regulation of daily life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually require eating at a communal table.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Compulsion to perform political functions is a legal burden with material time cost.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers broad political participation (voting, magistrates, assembly).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Persists as long as one remains in the 'rich' wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth brackets can change, though usually durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common practices in oligarchical states of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a known mechanism of oligarchical law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Richness (money) does not by definition mean 'legally forced to vote'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for high office (Archonship) grants political power/control.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers 'highest offices' and the 'majority of magistrates'.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exclusion persists as long as one remains below the wealth assessment.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth status is a durable but potentially changeable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes standard oligarchical practices known to the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reporting on the factual endeavor of specific constitutional types.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Poverty does not inherently mean legal exclusion from office in all systems.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Dowries are tangible physical wealth (money/land).
scores.generality_reasoning: Affects the broad transfer of wealth through marriage/family.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of a dowry has lifelong economic impact on an estate.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth status is a social identity, though generally durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a rule proposed by Phaleas, a previous legislator/theorist.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Analyzing a theoretical proposal rather than a broad social fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Richness does not conceptually forbid taking a dowry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land is the most tangible form of material property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Concerns the general ability to buy/sell/expand property.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Land limits or permanent holdings are lifelong structural features.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Specifically mentions the law at Locri and other states.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites specific historical/contemporary legislation (Locri).
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not automatically limit the amount of land one can buy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Personal freedom/ownership as a slave is the ultimate material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Enslavement affects all life outcomes and legal personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Public slavery is a lifelong structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Artisan status is an occupational identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a legislative proposal by Phaleas.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Interpreting the logic of a theoretical constitution (Phaleas).
scores.tautology_reasoning: An artisan is a craftsman; the definition does not include 'slave'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Food from land produce is a direct means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the entire maintenance/food source for a whole class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Structural support for the duration of military service/life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Soldier/Warrior is a role-bound or trained identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the system of Hippodamus of Miletus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a structural plan proposed by a specific theorist.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Soldiers are defined by fighting, not by eating common land produce.

scores.materiality_reasoning: State maintenance (pensions/provisions) is tangible economic aid.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific support for a recurring social situation (orphans).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Maintenance usually lasts throughout the recipient's childhood/minority.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage (being a child of a specific person) is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Explicitly states this law 'exists at present' in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Affirms the existence of the law as a present fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a war orphan does not inherently include state funding.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Evidentiary standards in murder trials affect life/freedom outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular legal procedure (murder trial).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource applies to a single legal episode (a trial).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a 'defendant' is a role-bound episodic status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites an existing law in the city of Cyme.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the law as a factual example of 'old laws'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Defendants are not by definition convicted by relatives' testimony.

scores.materiality_reasoning: External military alliance provides physical protection and political leverage.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the context of rebellions and diplomacy.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A stable convention affecting the group's long-term power.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Serf status (e.g. Helots/Penlestai) is structural and hereditary.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a 'hitherto occurred' stable practice in Crete.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the failure of cities to ally as an observed behavior.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a serf does not conceptually forbid receiving outside aid.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom from moral regulation relates to lifestyle and social control.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad scope of 'dissolute' living and daily conduct.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Constitutional neglect of half the population is lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex/Gender is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current state of laws in Sparta.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Author uses moral language ('dissolute') to describe a factual legal vacuum.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being female does not inherently mean 'exempt from state discipline'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Inheriting estates and owning 2/5 of the land is material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Land ownership is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Direct ownership of land and estates is lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex/Gender is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary property situation in Sparta.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific figures (2/5) as an observed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Women do not inherit land by definition (many societies forbid it).

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to choose a spouse determines property control and bodily autonomy.
scores.generality_reasoning: Marriage choice is a broad right affecting lineage and wealth.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Marriage and property transfer have lifelong effects.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Heiress status is determined by birth/lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes current Spartan legal regulations.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the factual legal allowance given to executors/fathers.
scores.tautology_reasoning: An heiress is not defined by 'lacking choice' (some could choose).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from all taxes is a direct material economic benefit.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers all taxes, a broad category of financial obligation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Persists once the threshold of children is reached.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Parental status (father of four) is a permanent biological fact.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a specific existing Spartan law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Neutral citation of a specific statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a father of four does not definitionally include tax immunity.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the Ephorate grants supreme political authority.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to one office, though it has 'absolute control'.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility lasts as long as one is a citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/membership in the 'entire people' is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current functioning of the Spartan state.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a standard factual procedure in Sparta.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not require being eligible for a supreme office.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting and citizenship determine legal personhood and political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizenship is the foundational axis of legal/political life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of citizenship is a lifelong structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'poor' status can change, though it is a durable bracket.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the 'ancestral' but still in-force Spartan regulation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific regulation about tax payment and citizenship.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Poverty does not definitionally exclude one from voting rights.
